title: walafrida angolensis
intro: Walafrida angolensis, also known as the African sandalwood or bushveld sandalwood, is a plant species native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oil, which contains sesquiterpenes and other compounds, and has been used to treat various health conditions. Walafrida angolensis is valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and skin irritations. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical application to harness its medicinal properties.
